events.title: Breakfast at Ya Kun Kaya Toast
events.description: Start your day with the iconic kaya toast and kopi set — a true Singaporean breakfast ritual since 1944.
events.location: Ya Kun Kaya Toast, Far East Square

events.title: Marina Bay Sands & Gardens by the Bay
events.description: Walk along the Marina Bay waterfront. Visit the Supertree Grove — free entry, stunning architecture.

events.title: Lunch at Maxwell Food Centre
events.description: Try the legendary Tian Tian Hainanese Chicken Rice — Michelin Bib Gourmand, under S$5.

events.title: Chinatown Heritage Walk
events.description: Explore Buddha Tooth Relic Temple, Chinatown Street Market, and the colourful shophouses.
events.culturalNudge: Remove shoes before entering the Buddha Tooth Relic Temple. Dress modestly — cover shoulders and knees.

events.title: Breathing Room
events.description: Cool down at a local café. Try an iced coffee at Tiong Bahru Bakery before the evening rush.

events.title: Clarke Quay Riverside Walk
events.description: Walk along the Singapore River. Watch the boats go by. Great vibe as the lights come on at sunset.

events.title: Dinner at Lau Pa Sat
events.description: One of Singapore's most iconic hawker centres in a beautiful Victorian-era cast iron structure. Try satay from Satay Street.

events.title: Marina Bay Sands Light Show
events.description: Watch the Spectra light and water show. Free. Best viewed from the waterfront promenade.

events.title: Breakfast at Toast Box
events.description: Traditional nanyang coffee and thick toast set — perfect fuel for a day of exploration.

events.title: Sentosa Island Adventure
events.description: Cable car to Sentosa. Visit the SEA Aquarium or just enjoy Palawan Beach — Southeast Asia's southernmost point.

events.title: Lunch at Vivocity Food Republic
events.description: Massive food court with everything from laksa to dim sum. Budget-friendly, air-conditioned.

events.title: Little India Walking Tour
events.description: Colourful shophouses, Sri Veeramakaliamman Temple, spice shops, and the best biryani in Singapore.
events.culturalNudge: Photography may be restricted inside Hindu temples. Ask before photographing worshippers.

events.title: Breathing Room
events.description: Decompress at a quiet café in Kampong Glam before the evening leg.

events.title: Orchard Road Shopping
events.description: Singapore's iconic shopping strip. Window shop at ION Orchard or browse local boutiques at Mandarin Gallery.

events.title: Dinner at Jumbo Seafood
events.description: Singapore's signature chilli crab — a must-try dish. Book ahead to avoid the queue.

events.title: Breakfast at Killiney Kopitiam
events.description: One of Singapore's oldest kopitiams. Try the kopi and French toast with kaya butter.

events.title: Botanic Gardens (UNESCO)
events.description: Singapore's UNESCO World Heritage Site. Explore the National Orchid Garden — 1,000+ orchid species.

events.title: Brunch at Tiong Bahru
events.description: Explore Singapore's hippest neighbourhood. Local bakeries, specialty coffee, indie bookshops.

events.title: National Gallery Singapore
events.description: Southeast Asia's largest modern art museum, housed in two magnificent heritage buildings.

events.title: Breathing Room
events.description: One last quiet moment before heading to the airport. Reflect on the trip at Merlion Park.

events.title: Last-Minute Shopping
events.description: Pick up souvenirs at Bugis Street Market — Singapore's largest street shopping destination.

events.title: Transfer to Airport
events.description: Head to Changi Airport. Allow 60 minutes for travel time.

documentChecklist: No visa needed for India passport holders (30-day free entry)
SIM card or data eSIM for connectivity
culturalNudges: Chewing gum is banned in Singapore — do not bring any
Jaywalking is a fineable offence — use pedestrian crossings
Durian is banned on public transport and in most hotels
Tipping is not expected — service charge already included
Remove shoes when entering temples and mosques

events.title: Breakfast at Leopold Café
events.description: Historic café in Colaba — vada pav, chai, and the buzzing energy of South Mumbai.

events.title: Gateway of India & Taj Mahal Palace
events.description: Mumbai's most iconic landmark. Walk around the harbour, photograph the Taj Hotel.

events.title: Lunch at Britannia & Co
events.description: Try the legendary berry pulao — a Parsi delicacy served since 1923.

events.title: Crawford Market & Chor Bazaar
events.description: Explore Mumbai's oldest and most vibrant markets. Spices, antiques, street life at its best.

events.title: Breathing Room
events.description: Cool down with a fresh coconut water on Marine Drive.

events.title: Marine Drive Sunset
events.description: Walk the Queen's Necklace as the sun sets. One of Mumbai's most iconic experiences.

events.title: Street Food at Chowpatty
events.description: Pav bhaji, sev puri, kulfi — the ultimate Mumbai street food experience.

events.title: South Indian Breakfast
events.description: Dosa, idli, and filter coffee at a traditional Udipi restaurant.

events.title: Elephanta Caves (UNESCO)
events.description: Take a ferry from Gateway of India to these ancient rock-cut cave temples.

events.title: Lunch at Bademiya
events.description: Mumbai's king of kebabs. Late-night legend, but great anytime.

events.title: Dhobi Ghat & Dharavi Walk
events.description: See the world's largest open-air laundry and take a respectful guided walk through Dharavi.

events.title: Breathing Room
events.description: Sit at a Bandra café and people-watch along Hill Road.

events.title: Bandra-Worli Sea Link & Bandstand
events.description: Drive across the sea link, then walk to Shah Rukh Khan's Mannat — an iconic Bandra landmark.

events.title: Dinner at Peshawri
events.description: Award-winning North Indian cuisine at ITC Grand Central.

events.title: Irani Chai & Bun Maska
events.description: Begin the last day with Mumbai's most beloved breakfast at Kyani & Co.

events.title: Chhatrapati Shivaji Terminus (UNESCO)
events.description: One of the world's most beautiful railway stations. Gothic-Victorian architecture at its finest.

events.title: Thali Lunch
events.description: Full Gujarati or Rajasthani thali — unlimited servings, incredible variety.

events.title: Linking Road & Hill Road Shopping
events.description: Bargain for clothes, accessories, and souvenirs in Bandra's famous shopping streets.

events.title: Breathing Room
events.description: Final quiet moment with a cutting chai at a street-side stall.

events.title: Transfer to Airport
events.description: Head to Chhatrapati Shivaji International Airport.

culturalNudges: Remove shoes before entering temples
Dress modestly at religious sites
Haggle respectfully at markets
Use right hand for greetings and eating
Respect photography restrictions at religious sites

events.title: English Breakfast
events.description: Full English at a traditional café — eggs, beans, toast, sausages, and a strong cup of tea.

events.title: Westminster & Big Ben
events.description: Walk past the Houses of Parliament, Big Ben, and Westminster Abbey.

events.title: Lunch at Borough Market
events.description: London's most famous food market. Try a steak sandwich or fresh oysters.

events.title: Tower of London
events.description: See the Crown Jewels and explore 1,000 years of history.

events.title: Breathing Room
events.description: Afternoon tea break at a riverside café near Tower Bridge.

events.title: South Bank Walk
events.description: Walk along the Thames from Tower Bridge to the Tate Modern.

events.title: Dinner
events.description: Enjoy dinner at a gastropub in Southwark.

events.title: Breakfast at Dishoom
events.description: Bombay-style café — bacon naan roll is legendary.

events.title: British Museum
events.description: Free entry. See the Rosetta Stone, Egyptian mummies, and Parthenon sculptures.

events.title: Lunch in Covent Garden
events.description: Choose from dozens of restaurants in London's liveliest piazza.

events.title: Oxford Street & Regent Street
events.description: London's premier shopping streets — from luxury flagships to high street stores.

events.title: Breathing Room
events.description: Relax in Hyde Park. People-watch near the Serpentine.

events.title: West End Show
events.description: Catch a musical or play in London's Theatre District.

events.title: Dinner in Soho
events.description: Late dinner in one of Soho's excellent restaurants.

events.title: Breakfast at The Wolseley
events.description: Elegant European-style café-restaurant on Piccadilly.

events.title: Buckingham Palace & St James's Park
events.description: Watch the Changing of the Guard (check schedule) then walk through St James's Park.

events.title: Lunch at a Pub
events.description: Traditional Sunday roast or fish and chips at a classic London pub.

events.title: Natural History Museum
events.description: Free entry. Iconic building and world-class exhibits.

events.title: Breathing Room
events.description: Final cuppa at a Kensington café before heading to the airport.

events.title: Transfer to Airport
events.description: Head to Heathrow Airport via the Piccadilly Line or Heathrow Express.

documentChecklist: UK visa if required (check visa requirements for your nationality)
Oyster card or contactless payment for transport
culturalNudges: Stand on the right side of escalators
Queue patiently — cutting queues is a serious faux pas
Tipping 10-15% is customary at restaurants
The Tube stops running around midnight (check Night Tube on Fri/Sat)

events.title: Breakfast at Tsukiji Outer Market
events.description: Fresh sushi, tamagoyaki, and matcha — the classic Tokyo morning start.

events.title: Senso-ji Temple, Asakusa
events.description: Tokyo's oldest and most famous temple. Walk through Nakamise-dori shopping street.
events.culturalNudge: Bow slightly before entering the temple gate. Do not touch the incense burner inside.

events.title: Ramen at Ichiran
events.description: Solo-booth ramen experience — customise every detail on a paper slip. Iconic.

events.title: Akihabara Electric Town
events.description: Anime, manga, electronics — Japan's otaku capital. Fun even if you're not a fan.

events.title: Breathing Room
events.description: Quiet matcha at a traditional tea house in Ueno.

events.title: Shibuya Crossing & Hachiko
events.description: The world's busiest pedestrian crossing. Walk it as the lights change. Visit the Hachiko statue.

events.title: Dinner in Shinjuku
events.description: Explore Memory Lane (Omoide Yokocho) — tiny yakitori bars under the train tracks.

events.title: Conbini Breakfast
events.description: Japanese convenience stores are incredible — try an onigiri, egg sandwich, and Boss coffee.

events.title: Meiji Shrine & Harajuku
events.description: Walk through the serene forested path to Meiji Shrine, then hit Takeshita Street for pop culture overload.
events.culturalNudge: Bow once before passing through the torii gate at Meiji Shrine. Keep your voice low inside the shrine grounds.

events.title: Crepes & Street Food in Harajuku
events.description: Rainbow cotton candy, giant crepes, and the most photogenic food in Tokyo.

events.title: TeamLab Borderless
events.description: Immersive digital art experience — one of the most visited museums in the world.

events.title: Breathing Room
events.description: Rest with an iced matcha latte at a Omotesando café.

events.title: Tokyo Tower at Sunset
events.description: Climb Tokyo Tower for panoramic city views as the sun sets.

events.title: Omakase Sushi
events.description: Multi-course chef's selection sushi at a traditional counter restaurant.

events.title: Hotel Breakfast
events.description: Japanese-style breakfast — grilled fish, miso soup, rice, pickles.

events.title: Imperial Palace East Gardens
events.description: Beautiful free gardens surrounding the Emperor's residence. Peaceful morning walk.

events.title: Tonkatsu Lunch
events.description: Crispy pork cutlet with cabbage and rice — a Tokyo comfort food staple.

events.title: Ginza Shopping
events.description: High-end shopping district. Browse Uniqlo's flagship, MUJI, and luxury boutiques.

events.title: Breathing Room
events.description: Final moment of zen at a quiet garden café.

events.title: Transfer to Airport
events.description: Take the Narita Express or Limousine Bus to the airport.

documentChecklist: Valid passport (no visa needed for many nationalities, 90-day stay)
Japan Rail Pass if planning day trips
Japanese Yen (JPY) — cash is still king in many places
culturalNudges: Never tip in Japan — it can be considered rude
Speak quietly on trains — phone calls are taboo
Always remove shoes when entering homes, temples, and many restaurants
Do not eat while walking — find a bench or stand still to eat
Bow when greeting — a slight nod is sufficient for tourists

events.title: Bagel Breakfast
events.description: Classic New York bagel with lox and cream cheese from a legendary deli.

events.title: Statue of Liberty & Ellis Island
events.description: Take the ferry to Liberty Island. Book the pedestal access for the best photo opportunity.

events.title: Pizza Lunch
events.description: Dollar slice or classic Margherita — you can't visit NYC without proper NY pizza.

events.title: 9/11 Memorial & One World Observatory
events.description: Pay respects at the memorial pools, then visit the observation deck for city views.

events.title: Breathing Room
events.description: Coffee and people-watching at Washington Square Park.

events.title: High Line Walk
events.description: Walk the elevated park built on a historic freight rail line. Stunning city views.

events.title: Dinner in Little Italy
events.description: Pasta, wine, and the charm of Mulberry Street.

events.title: Diner Breakfast
events.description: Classic American diner — pancakes, eggs, and bottomless coffee.

events.title: Central Park
events.description: Walk the Great Lawn, Bethesda Fountain, and Bow Bridge.

events.title: Lunch at Chelsea Market
events.description: Indoor food hall with everything from tacos to lobster rolls.

events.title: Metropolitan Museum of Art
events.description: One of the world's greatest art museums. Pay-what-you-wish for NY residents.

events.title: Breathing Room
events.description: Sit on the Met steps and watch Fifth Avenue go by.

events.title: Times Square & Broadway
events.description: The neon-lit heart of NYC. Catch a Broadway show if time allows.

events.title: Dinner in Koreatown
events.description: Korean BBQ on 32nd Street — grill your own meats and enjoy the vibrant K-Town energy.

events.title: Brunch
events.description: New York-style brunch — avocado toast, shakshuka, or eggs benedict.

events.title: Brooklyn Bridge Walk
events.description: Walk across the iconic bridge for stunning Manhattan skyline views. Start from the Brooklyn side.

events.title: DUMBO Food Scene
events.description: Grab a slice at Juliana's or explore the local food scene under the Manhattan Bridge.

events.title: SoHo Shopping
events.description: Designer boutiques, art galleries, and cast-iron architecture.

events.title: Breathing Room
events.description: Final coffee at a rooftop bar with city views.

events.title: Transfer to Airport
events.description: Head to JFK or Newark Airport via subway + AirTrain or taxi.

culturalNudges: Tip 18-20% at restaurants — it's not optional, it's expected
Walk fast and stay to the right on sidewalks
Don't block subway doors — let people exit first
Jaywalking is common but technically illegal
